name: Cloud Infrastructure SLA.pdf
text:
[Page 1]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
CTR-012  Cloud Infrastructure SLA
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 1
DOCUMENT CONTROL
Cloud Infrastructure SLA
Operational uptime and support commitments attached to the Northwind managed-service engagement.
Document ID
CTR-012
Owner
Vendor Management Office
Version
1.3
Effective Date
2024-01-15
Customer
Meridian Holdings Ltd.
Counterparty
Northwind Cloud Systems LLC
Synthetic corporate document for demo, evaluation, and retrieval workflows only. All parties, people, systems, repositories, and events are
fictional.
1. Service Commitments
Metric
Commitment
Measurement Window
Platform Availability
99.5% monthly uptime
Calendar month
Priority 1 Initial Response
15 minutes, 24×7
Per incident
Priority 2 Initial Response
1 hour, 24×7
Per incident
Priority 3 Initial Response
4 business hours
Per incident
Service Restoration Objective
Best efforts; target based on severity and
available workaround
Per incident
2. Severity Definitions and Update Cadence
Severity
Definition
Update Cadence
P1
Complete production outage or material compromise of the managed retrieval service.
Every 60 minutes
P2
Major degradation affecting critical workflows with no practical workaround.
Every 4 hours
P3
Material issue with available workaround or limited business effect.
Daily until stabilization
3. Service Credits
●
If monthly availability falls below 99.5% but remains at or above 99.0%, Customer receives a 5% service credit for the
affected month.
●
If monthly availability falls below 99.0% but remains at or above 98.5%, Customer receives a 10% service credit for the
affected month.
●
If monthly availability falls below 98.5%, Customer receives a 20% service credit. Service credits are the sole and
exclusive remedy for availability failures under this SLA.
4. Remedies and Liability

[Page 2]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
CTR-012  Cloud Infrastructure SLA
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 2
Clause 4.2 — Liability Cap. Provider’s aggregate liability arising out of or related to this SLA shall not exceed the fees paid
by Customer for the affected services during the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.
Clause 4.3 — No Renewal Mechanics. This SLA does not modify contract term, renewal, or non-renewal rights
established in the Master Service Agreement.
Clause 4.5 — Reporting. Provider shall deliver a monthly service report that includes availability calculations, incident
counts by severity, open corrective actions, and scheduled maintenance windows.
Primary review signal: 99.5% monthly uptime is materially below Meridian’s target 99.95% enterprise
baseline. This document also provides a capped-liability comparator against the unlimited MSA draft.
